docs.title: Alexander Skog
docs.text:
This site is a place for my notes on OpenShift, Kubernetes, virtualization, networking, and whatever else I end up learning while building things.

Most of what I write here is based on lab work, testing, and solving problems I run into myself. The goal is mostly to keep useful notes in one place and hopefully make them useful for someone else too.

docs.title: Posts
docs.text:
• Manage users with htpasswd
  • Running Squid as a container on OpenShift 4
  • Single-node OpenShift on OpenShift Virtualization

docs.title: What I want to add next
docs.text:
• more OpenShift troubleshooting notes
  • networking and proxy setup guides
  • homelab and virtualization write-ups

This site will probably stay pretty simple. I'd rather keep it practical than polish it too much.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
I'm Alexander, and I work mostly with infrastructure and platform-related topics.

This site is where I collect notes that I want to keep around, especially when I've spent time figuring something out and know I'll need it again later.

The topics I come back to most are:

  • OpenShift
  • Kubernetes
  • Virtualization
  • Networking
  • Troubleshooting and lab work

I’m not trying to make this into a polished content site. It’s mostly a personal knowledge base with write-ups that are a bit cleaner than raw notes.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For now, GitHub is the best place to find what I'm working on:

  • github.com/askog96

I may add more here later, but I want to keep this page minimal for now.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
A few things I want to write about when I have time:

  • OpenShift lab setup
  • DNS, ingress, and proxy configuration
  • Useful oc commands and troubleshooting steps
  • Lessons from running single-node OpenShift in a VM

This page is mostly here so I do not forget the topics I want to come back to.

docs.title: Create and Configure new users using htpassd
docs.text:
Create a new user new_admin with the password redhat in the existing file ~/auth-providers/htpasswd:

  htpasswd -c -B -b ~/auth-providers/htpasswd new_admin redhat

-c: Create a new file. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten. -B: Use bcrypt encryption for the password, which is more secure. -b: Use the password from the command line rather than prompting for it. ~/auth-providers/htpasswd: The path to the file where the user credentials will be stored. new_admin: The username of the new user. redhat: The password for the new user.

⚠ Caution: Running this command with the `-c` option will overwrite the existing htpasswd file. Make sure you do not lose existing user data.

docs.title: Create new secret used by OAuth
docs.text: oc create secret generic localusers --from-file htpasswd=~/auth-providers/htpasswd -n openshift-config

docs.title: Add a user to existing OAuth config
Extract the file data from secret
Add new user to htpasswd called manager with password redhat
Running squidproxy as a container on OpenShift 4
docs.text:
After I have been running squidproxy on a physical machine, I decided to give it a try to run it as a container.

  htpasswd -c -B -b ~/auth-providers/htpasswd new_admin redhat
⚠ Caution: Running this command with the `-c` option will overwrite the existing htpasswd file. Make sure you do not lose existing user data.

Single node OpenShift running on OpenShift Virtualization
docs.text: A quite large project to get Single node OpenShift to run inside a VM on OpenShift Virtualization. The intention is to more effectivly using the hardware but also enabling an aaS concept with Single node OpenShift

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before proceeding, its recommened to use the following: - ## Hardware: Ensure that your system is running one of the following: - Any server with atleast 192GB of memory. - Intel Xenon 6132 CPU - Intel xxv710 or Intel E810 NIC

  • Admin rights to install required software

  • Access to the project repository

  • Stable internet connection

  • Access to required internal/external resources

  • Docker (if containerization is used)
  • Virtual environment (recommended for Python projects)
  htpasswd -c -B -b ~/auth-providers/htpasswd new_admin redhat
⚠ Caution: Running this command with the `-c` option will overwrite the existing htpasswd file. Make sure you do not lose existing user data.

docs.title: Operating System:
docs.text:
• Red Hat OpenShift 4.15 or later.
  • OpenShift Virtualization x.zz
  • List of operators TBA
